meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide (Advanced Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with SaaS architecture. Master API integrations as a freelancer using this advanced checklist, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch. Use Antigravity to scaffold your API layer and Claude Code to automate the implementation. By abstracting the integration logic into modular, reusable service classes, you eliminate architectural debt, ensure type safety, and deliver enterprise-grade SaaS features to your clients in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your sprint, your client is asking for a complex third-party integration, and your current codebase feels like a house of cards. You're trapped in the 'no SaaS knowledge' loop, spending hours debugging brittle endpoints instead of shipping value. It’s the silent killer of freelance profit margins: wasted time on reinventing the wheel.

But what if you could treat API integrations as a utility, not a roadblock? By leveraging AI-assisted scaffolding and intelligent CLI agents, you can bypass the manual drudgery. This guide provides the blueprint to transform from a stressed developer into an AI-augmented architect, building robust, scalable integrations that make your clients wonder how you move so fast.

steps.title: Define the API Contract with Antigravity
steps.description: Before writing code, define your payload schemas and endpoints in Antigravity to generate clean, typed service stubs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript service class for a REST API integration. Include input validation using Zod, error handling for 4xx/5xx responses, and a retry strategy with exponential backoff.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate API interface based on OpenAPI spec provided in ./schema.yaml'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate with Claude Code
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate. Use Claude Code to map your Antigravity-generated stubs to your existing database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mapper function that transforms external API response objects into our internal database models, ensuring all null fields are handled safely.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add ./src/services/api-client.ts --instruction 'Implement mapping logic for the user profile endpoint'

steps.title: Implement Middleware for Authentication
steps.description: Secure your integrations by abstracting auth tokens into a centralized middleware, preventing repetitive configuration across your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Axios interceptor that injects Bearer tokens from environment variables and handles token refresh logic automatically.

steps.title: Deploy Resilient Error Handling
steps.description: Wrap all API calls in a circuit breaker pattern to ensure your app doesn't crash when third-party services fail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a Circuit Breaker pattern for external API calls, logging failures to a custom monitoring endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap all service calls in a circuit breaker wrapper'

steps.title: Validation and Contract Testing
steps.description: Verify your integration against real-world scenarios using automated test suites generated by AI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the API service class, mocking successful responses and common error scenarios like rate limiting.

pro_tips: Always use Zod for runtime type checking on API responses—never trust external JSON payloads.
Cache API responses in Redis with short TTLs to reduce latency and save on API consumption costs for your client.
Use a dedicated 'Integration Layer' folder structure to keep external business logic separated from your core app logic.
Implement request logging that strips PII (Personally Identifiable Information) before hitting your logs for easier debugging.
Version your API service classes (e.g., v1, v2) to prevent breaking existing client features during updates.
content.next_steps: Set up a CI/CD pipeline to run your generated unit tests on every commit, ensuring your API integrations remain stable as your client's needs evolve.
content.conversion_block: Don't let technical debt dictate your freelance career. Build faster, charge more, and deliver excellence with every commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
